mission.title: litellm: [Bug]: OpenAI GPT-5 Chat model does not support "temperature" parameter
mission.description: [Bug]: OpenAI GPT-5 Chat model does not support "temperature" parameter. Users encounter: "`litellm.UnsupportedParamsError: gpt-5 models don't support temperature=0.3. Only temperature=1 is s".

steps.title: Identify litellm troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your litellm deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm -n litellm --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: ``litellm.UnsupportedParamsError: gpt-5 models don't support temperature=0.3. Only temperature=1 is supported. To drop un`

steps.title: Review litellm configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant litellm configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm
kubectl get configmap -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=litellm
```
### What happened?

When using the **gpt-5-chat-latest** model, an intelligence model, it throws a LiteLLM error:

`litellm.UnsupportedParamsError: gpt-5 models don't support temperature=0.3. Only temperature=1 is supported.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Bug]: OpenAI GPT-5 Chat model does not support…
steps.description:
## Support temperature for GPT-5 Chat model

GPT-5 chat is not an inference model, and since it requires support for parameters like temperature or tool_choice that are unavailable in inference models, I modified gpt_5_transformation to apply only when the model is a reasoning model.

Note: Since
```yaml
import os
import openai

client = openai.OpenAI(
    api_key=os.getenv("litellm_api_key"),
    base_ur=os.getenv("litellm_url")
)

client.chat.completions.create(
    model="openai/gpt-5-chat-latest",
    messages=[
        {
            "role": "user",
            "content": "What is the capital of México?",
        },
    ],
    temperature=0.3,
)
```

steps.title: Confirm [Bug]: OpenAI GPT-5 Chat model does not support… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm -n litellm --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n litellm --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that ``litellm.UnsupportedParamsError: gpt-5 models don't support temperature=0.3. Only temperature=1 is supported. To drop un` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary:
## Support temperature for GPT-5 Chat model

GPT-5 chat is not an inference model, and since it requires support for parameters like temperature or tool_choice that are unavailable in inference models, I modified gpt_5_transformation to apply only when the model is a reasoning model.
codeSnippets: litellm.UnsupportedParamsError: gpt-5 models don't support temperature=0.3. Only temperature=1 is supported. To drop unsupported params set `litellm.drop_params = True`

litellm_params:
      model: openai/gpt-5-chat-latest
      api_key: os.environ/OPENAI_API_KEY
      allowed_openai_params: ["temperature"]

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with litellm installed or the issue environment reproducible.
